dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic edged the day because Claude-related discussion stayed mixed rather than collapsing into a single negative narrative: one front-page thread largely rejected a suspicious Claude metric as a base-rate mistake [E2], one commenter gave detailed practical praise for Claude Code in real infrastructure work [E4], and the main knock was a separate complaint about Claude Code’s issue tracker behavior [E3].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic finished first not on a wave of hype, but by avoiding the sharper backlash that hit others. Hacker News had one neutralizing story about Claude-linked GitHub activity where top comments pushed back on the premise [E2], one concrete positive report that Claude Code handled setup, verification, documentation, and maintenance tasks well [E4], and one concrete negative complaint about auto-closing bugs in Claude Code’s tracker [E3]. That left Anthropic with the broadest mixed-but-usable signal, while OpenAI’s day was dragged down by disappointment around Sora’s shutdown [E1] and Microsoft Copilot’s by anger over default data-use settings [E6].

openai.judgementSnippet: Sora’s shutdown drove a distinctly sour OpenAI discussion: commenters said the product’s novelty faded quickly, criticized its feed-like/addictive direction, and thought the abrupt closure after fresh safety messaging made the company look inconsistent [E1].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude discussion split three ways: the low-star-repo story was heavily challenged as a base-rate fallacy rather than taken as proof of weak adoption [E2], one DNS self-hosting comment praised Claude Code’s hands-on usefulness in setup and verification [E4], and another thread complained that Claude Code’s bug tracker auto-closes issues and leaves problems lingering [E3].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: GitHub Copilot’s policy update landed badly: top comments objected to default opt-out training, worried about business-account implications, and argued sensitive files can be swept up just by opening an IDE [E6].

evidence.summary: The 'Goodbye to Sora' thread mixed some early-fun nostalgia with stronger disappointment: top comments said the novelty wore off, disliked the app’s meme-feed/addictive framing, and thought the abrupt shutdown looked bad for OpenAI.

In '90% of Claude-linked output going to GitHub repos w <2 stars,' leading comments mostly rejected the negative implication, calling it a base-rate fallacy and saying GitHub stars are a weak proxy for value or usage.
An Apple bug-report thread included a direct complaint about Claude Code’s GitHub issue tracker: a commenter said a bot auto-closes vaguely related issues and that some bugs remain unfixed for years.

In 'You can run a DNS server,' a top comment gave detailed praise for Claude Code, saying it could set up and verify DNS chains, test firewall/ports, automate updates and backups, and document the infrastructure 'like a charm.'

The GitHub Copilot interaction-data policy update drew backlash over default-enabled training, opt-out framing, questions about business defaults, and fears that sensitive files can be sent to Microsoft by default.
